@graph.name: BMW Z4 sDrive28i / 30i Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW Z4 sDrive28i / 30i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Z4 sDrive28i (N20) engine typically costs £3,500–£6,500 supply only, while a reconditioned sDrive30i (B48) engine costs £4,500–£8,500. Fitting adds £800–£1,500 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW Z4 sDrive28i / 30i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which generation you're asking about. The N20 (E89 sDrive28i, 2011–2016) has a well-documented timing chain tensioner issue — pre-2015 cars are the highest risk, and the water pump recall is a safety-critical item. The B48 (G29 sDrive30i, 2019+) is generally considered reliable, but oil and coolant leaks are common. Across both, oil-change discipline is everything.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW Z4 sDrive28i / 30i?

acceptedAnswer.text: N20 timing chain wear (E89), N20 water pump recall (E89 — fire risk), B48 oil leaks from valve cover and oil filter housing (G29), B48 timing chain guide fragility (G29), and B48 coolant leaks (G29). See the Era Map above for generation-specific advice.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW Z4 sDrive28i / 30i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept E89 Z4 (values £6,000–£15,000+), a £4,000–£5,000 recon is 30–60% of the car's value — borderline but often worth it for a good example. Against a £20,000–£40,000 G29, a £5,000–£6,000 recon is 15–25% — worth it. The BMW scrap threshold of 70% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW Z4 sDrive28i / 30i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E89 sDrive28i (2011–2016) = N20B20A; G29 sDrive30i (2019–present) = B48B20. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N20 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain, tensioner and guide replacement costs £800–£2,000. BMW extended the warranty on N20 timing chains to 7 years/70,000 miles in some markets. However, if the chain snaps, a full engine replacement (£3,500–£6,500) is usually required. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW Z4 sDrive28i / 30i Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current Z4 sDrive30i B48 era. Older generations: see the Era Map above for E89/N20-specific pricing. A replacement engine for a BMW can cost anywhere from £1,000 to over £5,000, with typical reconditioned engine costs ranging from £1,200–£2,200 supply only.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Z4 engine typically takes 8–12 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). The Z4's engine bay is reasonably accessible. [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

cta.label: → Compare Z4 sDrive28i / 30i engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: N20 Timing Chain & Tensioner Failure (E89 sDrive28i, pre-2015)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or whining noise from the engine on cold start, metallic clattering, engine warning light, loss of power, potential engine failure if chain skips or snaps.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — the timing chain can develop slack, causing rapid wear on sprockets and plastic guides.
problems.replacementCost: £3,500–£6,500 (if chain snaps and valves meet pistons)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The N20's timing chain tensioner is the defining weak point of this engine generation. A failing tensioner can lead to a rattling noise from the engine, and if it completely fails, it can cause catastrophic engine damage. BMW extended the warranty on N20 timing chains to 7 years/70,000 miles in some markets due to the severity of the issue. Pre-2015 cars are the highest risk. A cold-start rattle is the early warning sign — ignore it and you risk piston-valve contact and a full engine replacement. At the first sign of rattle, get it inspected immediately.

problems.title: N20 Water Pump & Electrical Short-Circuit Recall (E89 sDrive28i)
problems.symptoms: Engine warning light, coolant loss, potential engine fire risk, water pump electrical connector failure.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — recall affecting 2012–2016 Z4 sDrive28i models
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless overheating leads to engine damage)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: check recall status immediately; can cause engine fire

problems.recommendation: BMW recalled more than 720,000 vehicles, including the 2012–2016 Z4 sDrive28i, due to a fire risk involving the N20 engine. The N20 engine could short-circuit at the water pump's electrical plug connector due to accumulating blow-by fluid from the positive crankcase ventilation system. If your Z4 sDrive28i hasn't had this recall work completed, check your vehicle's recall status immediately. This is a safety-critical item — do not delay.

problems.title: B48 Oil Leaks (G29 sDrive30i — Valve Cover & Oil Filter Housing)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, visible oil seepage around the valve cover or oil filter housing, low oil level warning.
problems.repairCost: £400–£900 (valve cover gasket or oil filter housing gasket replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; oil leaks can damage other components

problems.recommendation: Oil leaks are one of the most common complaints with the B48 engine, particularly from the valve cover gasket and oil filter housing. Over time, the gaskets can wear out, leading to leaks that can affect engine performance. If you notice oil spots under the car or a burning smell, have it inspected promptly. Valve cover gasket and oil filter housing gaskets can get brittle over time.

problems.title: B48 Timing Chain Guide Fragility (G29 sDrive30i)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise from the engine, potential timing chain jump, engine warning light, loss of power.
problems.replacementCost: £4,500–£8,500 (if chain jumps and valves meet pistons)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The B48 engine is generally considered reliable, but the timing chain guide can become fragile around 60,000 km, with a risk of chain jumping and engine damage. If you hear a rattling noise from the engine, have the timing system inspected immediately. Regular oil changes with the correct BMW-approved oil are the best prevention.

problems.title: B48 Coolant Leaks (G29 sDrive30i)
problems.symptoms: Low coolant warning, coolant loss with no visible external leak, sweet smell from engine bay, temperature fluctuations, overheating.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless overheating leads to engine damage)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant loss is detected

problems.recommendation: Coolant leaks are a common issue on modern BMW engines, with the heater management module and oil filter housing being weak points that can lead to overheating and expensive repairs. The EGR radiator can also suffer from coolant leaks. If you notice coolant loss or temperature fluctuations, have the system pressure-tested immediately. Proactive replacement of known weak coolant hoses at 60,000 miles is recommended.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW Z4 sDrive28i / 30i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — far cheaper than engine replacement
Recall work is free; out-of-warranty repair is still viable
Catch it early — far cheaper than engine replacement
Diagnosis and repair is straightforward; catch it before overheating
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Z4 sDrive28i / 30i? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with oil changes at 8,000–10,000 mile intervals
* E89 (2011–2016): timing chain tensioner replaced or inspected (post-2015 cars are lower risk)
* E89 (2011–2016): water pump recall completed (check with BMW)
* G29 (2019+): no coolant loss and cooling system in good order
* No cold-start rattle on any generation
avoidIf: * No proof of regular oil changes — these engines are unforgiving of neglect
* E89 with cold-start rattle — timing chain work is imminent and expensive
* E89 with no proof of water pump recall — potential fire risk
* G29 with coolant loss with no diagnosis — cooling system repairs can be complex
* Any engine warning light without diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive faults

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW Z4 sDrive28i and sDrive30i represent two generations of BMW's iconic roadster. The E89 sDrive28i introduced BMW's new turbocharged four-cylinder, replacing the beloved straight-six — it offers strong performance but the N20 timing chain tensioner is a critical weak point, and the water pump recall is a safety-critical item. The G29 sDrive30i is a significant step forward in reliability, with the B48 engine offering 255–258 bhp and 295 lb-ft of torque, but coolant and oil leaks are the key watchpoints. Across both generations, one rule applies: oil-change discipline is everything. BMW's extended service intervals are widely considered inadequate — treat your Z4 to 8,000–10,000 mile oil changes and it will reward you with hundreds of thousands of miles of driving pleasure.

cta.label: → Compare Z4 sDrive28i / 30i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW Z4 sDrive28i or sDrive30i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle lasting more than 1–2 seconds (N20 timing chain warning; B48 timing chain guide warning)
* Service History — Including which engine era (N20/B48) and confirmed oil-change discipline (8k–10k miles max)
* Water Pump Recall (E89) — Check recall status; this is a safety-critical item on the N20
* Oil Leaks (G29) — Check for oil around the valve cover and oil filter housing
* Coolant Level — Check cold and hot; any loss indicates potential cooling system issues
* Engine Temperature — Any fluctuations? Cooling system may be failing
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Timing, cooling, or oil leak codes are most common
* MOT History — Check for emissions failures (often related to fuel system or PCV issues)

items.value: 1. How much does a BMW Z4 sDrive28i / 30i engine replacement cost in the UK?

items.value: 2. Is the BMW Z4 sDrive28i / 30i engine reliable?

items.label: It depends on which generation you're asking about. The N20 (E89 sDrive28i, 2011–2016) has a well-documented timing chain tensioner issue — pre-2015 cars are the highest risk, and the water pump recall is a safety-critical item. The B48 (G29 sDrive30i, 2019+) is generally considered reliable, with the B48-powered Z4 well-regarded for its powertrain, but oil and coolant leaks are common. Across both, oil-change discipline is everything.
items.value: 3. What are the common problems with the BMW Z4 sDrive28i / 30i?

items.label: N20 timing chain wear (E89), N20 water pump recall (E89 — fire risk), B48 oil leaks from valve cover and oil filter housing (G29), B48 timing chain guide fragility (G29), and B48 coolant leaks (G29). See the Era Map above for generation-specific advice.
items.value: 4. Is it worth replacing a BMW Z4 sDrive28i / 30i engine, or should I scrap the car?

items.label: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept E89 Z4 (values £6,000–£15,000+), a £4,000–£5,000 recon is 30–60% of the car's value — borderline but often worth it for a good example. Against a £20,000–£40,000 G29, a £5,000–£6,000 recon is 15–25% — worth it. The BMW scrap threshold of 70% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing.
items.value: 5. How do I find my BMW Z4 sDrive28i / 30i engine code?

items.label: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E89 sDrive28i (2011–2016) = N20B20A; G29 sDrive30i (2019–present) = B48B20. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.
items.value: 6. Can I repair the N20 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

items.label: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain, tensioner and guide replacement costs £800–£2,000. BMW extended the warranty on N20 timing chains to 7 years/70,000 miles in some markets. However, if the chain snaps, a full engine replacement (£3,500–£6,500) is usually required. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.
